name: Pecan Tarts
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unsalted butter, very cold and cut into 1/2-inch pieces
2/3 cup Whipping cream, at room temperature
2 tablespoons Unsalted butter, at room temperature
recipeInstructions:
1. To make tart shells, place flour, cold butter, 3 tablespoons sugar, and salt in a food processor. Process using quick on and off motion until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
2. In a small bowl, combine 2 egg yolks and 1 tablespoon cream. Add this to the flour mixture while the motor is running. Process just until dough begins to form.
3. Add enough remaining cream to form dough that holds together. Scrape dough onto a piece of foil, wrap tightly, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
4. Divide dough into 6 or 8 equal portions based on pan size. Roll each portion out onto lightly floured pastry cloth.
5. Carefully transfer pastry to tart pans, pressing against bottoms and sides, then trim excess around rims. If the pastry tears, patch with small bits of pastry trimmings.
6. Refrigerate pastry-lined tart pans until ready to use.
1. To make caramel sauce, heat 1/2 cup sugar in a small, heavy skillet or saucepan over medium heat until melted and medium amber in color. Carefully pour in 2/3 cup cream all at once, avoiding splatters. Stir in 2 tablespoons butter. Cook, stirring constantly, over medium heat until the sauce is smooth. Remove from heat and store at room temperature if not using immediately.
7. To make filling, combine corn syrup, 3/4 cup each sugar and brown sugar, 4 eggs, and 2 egg yolks in a medium bowl. Stir until blended.
8. Heat 3 tablespoons butter in a small skillet over medium heat until foamy and light brown. Remove from heat and whisk into the corn syrup mixture.
9. Assemble tarts by setting pastry-lined tart pans on a large baking sheet. Divide pecans among the tart pans and pour filling over them, or pour filling and arrange pecans in a symmetrical pattern.
10. Bake at 375°F for 35 to 40 minutes. Remove from wire rack to cool. Serve tarts at room temperature topped with caramel sauce. Makes 6 to 8 tarts.
